name: Lot Valley Cycle Route: Aiguillon and Clairac Loop
name_original: Le confluent du Lot et de la Garonne — boucle
description: This 27.3-mile (44.0 km) difficult touring cycling loop around Aiguillon offers challenging climbs, river views, and charming French village
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Aiguillon are characterized by the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, offering a unique aquatic landscape. The region features the tranquil Lot Valley, known for its varied terrain and picturesque waterways. Cyclists can explore rolling countryside, gentle hills, and orchards, providing a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Aiguillon The most popular touring cycling route is The confluence of the Lot and the Garonne — loop, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the unique meeting point of the two major rivers. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Castelmoron-sur-Lot – Lot Valley cycle route loop from Aiguillon, a moderate 29.0 miles (46.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Lot Valley and passes through charming rural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Baïse Aqueduct loop from Aiguillon, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail leading through the Canal Latéral à la Garonne, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Aiguillon is defined by the Lot and Garonne rivers, the Lot Valley, and rolling countryside with orchards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aiguillon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aiguillon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aiguillon?
A: There are nearly 100 touring cycling routes around Aiguillon, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Aiguillon?
A: The terrain around Aiguillon is characterized by the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, offering a unique aquatic landscape. You can expect to cycle through the tranquil Lot Valley, featuring rolling countryside, gentle hills, and orchards. Routes often provide a mix of flat sections along the rivers and canals, interspersed with moderate ascents through the rural landscape.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Aiguillon?
A: Yes, Aiguillon offers a good selection of easy touring cycling routes. There are 24 routes specifically categorized as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10609904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lot Valley cycle route – Lot Valley Cycle Route loop from Lacaze&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 18 miles (29 km) with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Aiguillon has 38 routes categorized as difficult. These routes often feature more significant elevation changes and longer distances. A notable challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287109552/the-confluence-of-the-lot-and-the-garonne-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The confluence of the Lot and the Garonne — loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27.3 miles (44 km) long and includes over 1,800 feet (575 m) of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Aiguillon?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Aiguillon are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287109552/the-confluence-of-the-lot-and-the-garonne-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The confluence of the Lot and the Garonne — loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35259531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castelmoron-sur-Lot – Lot Valley cycle route loop from Aiguillon&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your cycling adventures.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Aiguillon?
A: While cycling around Aiguillon, you'll encounter the significant confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, which defines the region's landscape. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, a historic waterway. The area is rich in charming rural landscapes, orchards, and picturesque villages like Prayssas, which has a notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/598317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Village Square&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, several points of interest can be found along the touring cycling routes. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buzet-sur-Baïse Lock&lt;/a&gt; on the Canal Latéral à la Garonne, or historical religious buildings like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Christopher's Church in Vianne&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the 13th-century bastide town of Aiguillon itself, offering historical charm.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Aiguillon?
A: The touring cycling routes in Aiguillon are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil riverine landscapes, the charming rural scenery, and the well-maintained paths that offer a pleasant mix of flat sections and gentle climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Aiguillon?
A: The Lot-et-Garonne region, where Aiguillon is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through early autumn. Spring brings blooming orchards and mild temperatures, while autumn provides comfortable weather and beautiful fall foliage. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are often preferred for cycling during this season.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Aiguillon?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, the 24 easy routes around Aiguillon, often featuring flatter sections along rivers and canals, are generally suitable for families. These routes allow for a more relaxed pace and can be a great way to explore the scenic countryside together.

Q: What are some of the unique landscapes I'll encounter?
A: The most unique landscape feature is the confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers, providing a distinctive aquatic environment. You'll also cycle through the picturesque Lot Valley, characterized by its tranquil waterways, extensive orchards, and gently rolling countryside. This blend offers a diverse and engaging backdrop for your rides.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: Yes, the riverine environment of the Lot and Garonne, along with the surrounding rural landscapes, provides opportunities for wildlife spotting. Keep an eye out for various bird species along the waterways and in the quieter countryside areas. The tranquil nature of many routes makes them ideal for observing local fauna.
tips:
Aiguillon is at the confluence of the Lot and the Garonne. In addition to a pretty town centre, it has a very interesting museum dedicated to a local painter, Raoul Dastrac.

Every October, the International Watercolor and Travel Diary Festival takes place within the town and in this museum.

For those who love cycling, it's nice to see that travel stories are given pride of place.
Beach on the Lot, supervised swimming in summer. Picnic tables nearby.

Like many small towns located along the Lot River, Clairac has its own beach. But it also boasts some of the region's most beautiful half-timbered houses, as well as the Saint-Pierre-ès-Liens church, known for its remarkable maître d'hôtel.
Built in the 11th century next to the castle built around the same time, this church overlooks the Garonne and Port-Sainte-Marie.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated riding time for The confluence of the Lot and the Garonne — loop is approximately 2 hours and 52 minutes. This duration is for active cycling and does not include stops for sightseeing, breaks, or exploring the charming villages along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly paved, offering a smooth surface for touring bicycles. However, despite the paved surfaces, it is rated as 'difficult' due to significant and sustained climbs, with an elevation gain of approximately 575 meters. Be prepared for ascents that can be demanding, especially if you are carrying luggage.

faqs.question: Is this cycling loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This loop is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its significant elevation changes. While the region offers many family-friendly and flatter sections, this specific 44-kilometer route with its 575 meters of climbing is generally not recommended for beginners or young families looking for an easy ride. More experienced touring cyclists will find it rewarding.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers a rich blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. You'll pass through picturesque agricultural landscapes with orchards, vineyards, and fields of corn or sunflowers. Key highlights include the town of Aiguillon, the Aiguillon Beach on the Lot River, and the Historic Center of Clairac. The confluence of the Lot and Garonne rivers near Nicole is also a notable natural spectacle.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact loop are not detailed, Aiguillon serves as a central point for the route. You can typically find parking within the town of Aiguillon, which is well-connected to the broader Lot Valley Cycle Route. Look for public parking facilities near the town center or along the river.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region is beautiful during spring (April-May) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is generally mild and the landscapes are vibrant with blossoms or harvest colors. Summer can be very hot, making the climbs more challenging. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the cycling path?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on public cycling paths in France, provided they are kept on a leash and under control. Please ensure you clean up after your pet. Be mindful of agricultural areas and local regulations, especially when passing through villages.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this loop. It utilizes public roads and designated cycle paths that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the loop intersect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the "La Vallée du Lot à vélo" (25% of the route) and the "Voie de Vézelay Est, Clairac - Montréal-du-Gers" (13% of the route). It also briefly touches upon the "Véloroute de la Vallée du Lot (Damazan - Aiguillon)".

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages, including Aiguillon and Clairac, where you can find cafes, bakeries, and restaurants. Given the focus on 'Lot Valley lifestyle' and local produce, you'll likely encounter opportunities to enjoy regional gastronomy. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and elevation, essential items include a well-maintained touring bicycle, appropriate cycling gear, a helmet, and plenty of water. Consider packing snacks, sun protection, basic repair tools, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone. Layers of clothing are advisable for changing weather conditions, and a map or GPS device is recommended for navigation.
